purpose: Given a corpus token (from the Nawawī40 ḥadīth set or a Qurʾān sample), assert the correct authoring CLASSIFICATION against the existing Qamus and the shape of the candidate it should produce. This is the gate for the diff step that feeds new content into Qamus: a token is routed to exactly one bucket, and the expected candidate shape (entry vs occurrence-augment vs particle) is pinned. Mirrors the conservative diff used in ../../corpora/nawawi40/out/nawawi40.refine_summary.json — automated weak-root tying is ~50% precision, so any 'new_root' assertion still demands QAC/human confirmation.

classes.already_in_qamus: lemma + root already have a published entry — author NOTHING new; at most attach this occurrence
classes.occurrence_augment: entry exists; add this surface/āyah occurrence to an existing sense (no new entry, no new sense)
classes.new_surface: lemma exists but THIS inflected surface is not yet mapped to it — map surface→lemma, no new entry
classes.new_lemma: root exists in Qamus but this lemma (e.g. a different derivation/form) is new — new entry under the existing root
classes.new_root: neither root nor lemma present — new root + entry; weak/hamza roots require QAC or human confirmation before asserting radicals
classes.particle: closed-set particle or construction — rootless; goes to the particle spine, never gets a content root

candidate_shape_ref: expected_candidate fields follow ../../qamus/schemas/qamus-entry.schema.json (status=candidate) and ../../qamus/schemas/authored-gloss.schema.json for occurrence-level rows. Public record is always {src:'qamus',kind:'authored',lang:'en'}; source_scope names the corpus (quran|nawawi40) as INTERNAL provenance only.

token.surface_ar: vowelled surface as it appears in the corpus

case_schema.norm_key: the over-collapsed key the differ sees
case_schema.expected_candidate: the shape the differ should emit for this class
case_schema.evidence_required: qac | norm_strict | content_letter_harakah | hamza_seat | shadda | wazn | context_nahw | referent | existing_entry
case_schema.note: why this bucket and not its neighbours

expected_candidate.note: strip the بِ + ٱل + plural ـات; map the broken/sound plural surface to the singular lemma نِيَّة

cases.note: root ن و ي may already exist via the verb نَوَى; the NOMINAL lemma نِيَّة is the new entry. Do not let بِٱلـ proclitics or the plural ـات leak into the headword.

expected_candidate.note: عَمَل already published; attach this plural occurrence to its existing sense

cases.note: broken plural أَعْمَال has no surface affinity to عَمَل — match by lemma, then augment; creating a second entry would duplicate.

expected_candidate.note: strip the ـهُ possessive enclitic; the maṣdar هِجْرَة is the lemma even if the verb هَجَرَ root exists

cases.note: the enclitic ـهُ is not part of the lemma; maṣdar vs finite verb is a sense distinction that needs the second vote.

expected_candidate.note: route to the particle spine; the sukūn on the nūn + no kasra on the mīm distinguishes it from مِن 'from'

cases.note: rootless closed-set particle; absent the harakāt the مَن/مِن split cannot be auto-decided, so the spine assignment itself is pending until vowels confirm.

expected_candidate.note: published Form-IV maṣdar entry already exists; do not re-author. At most record the nawawi40 occurrence as internal provenance.

cases.note: the س ل م polyseme quarantine already governs إِسْلَام 'submission' vs سَلَام 'peace'; the existing entry must not be split by a second authoring pass.

expected_candidate.note: tāʾ marbūṭa is part of the lemma, not a suffix; the genitive kasra is case-marking, strip it

cases.note: root ش ه د likely present via شَهِدَ; the maṣdar شَهَادَة is the new nominal lemma.

expected_candidate.note: non-Arabic proper noun — assign NO content root; never derive radicals from the surface

cases.note: a name is not its root; proper nouns need a referent/context check, and foreign names get no triliteral root at all.

senses.gloss: to keep (one's side) away from / forsake (the bed)

expected_candidate.note: Form VI تَفَاعَلَ of a final-weak root; the ى is the weak radical — root candidate ج ف و must be QAC-confirmed, not asserted from the surface

cases.note: weak-final + derived form: the conservative differ flags this as new_root but the ~50%-precision weak-root tie means radicals are NOT asserted without confirmation.

expected_candidate.note: published maṣdar 'praise' entry exists; the ٱل is the definite article, not part of the lemma

cases.note: high-frequency, single-sense, key-clean once the article is stripped — no authoring.

expected_candidate.note: the kasra is iḍāfa case-marking on the existing lemma رَبّ; map the inflected surface, do not create an entry

cases.note: lemma exists; only the case-inflected surface رَبِّ needs to be tied to it. Distinguish from the doubled-root trap (ر ب vs ر ب ب) via the shadda.

expected_candidate.note: construction = the إِيَّا support + 2ms enclitic; rootless. norm سees an اياك-shaped key but there is no triliteral stem

cases.note: do not certify a root from a pronoun-support construction; route to the particle/pronoun spine.

expected_candidate.note: Form X ٱسْتَفْعَلَ of a hollow root; the ـعِينُ surfaces the weak middle radical و — root ع و ن is QAC-confirmed, not read off the surface ي

cases.note: hollow + Form X: the imperfect prefix نَـ and the istifʿāl frame must be peeled before the root is even guessed; weak-root precision demands confirmation.

expected_candidate.note: strip ٱل + accusative fatḥa; concrete noun, single sense

cases.note: a clean new nominal lemma; two-vote is the standard floor for any new entry even when the surface is unambiguous.

expected_candidate.root: ع م م? -> ع ل م
expected_candidate.guard: must attach to the ع ل م 'All-Knowing' entry, NOT the أ ل م 'painful' data-error key
expected_candidate.note: augment the existing عَلِيم entry; the nominative tanwīn must not spawn a separate entry or re-trip the quarantine

cases.note: ties the regression (see ./qamus-regression-eval.json#aleem-all-knowing-not-painful) into the authoring path: an occurrence of a quarantined homograph augments the CORRECT entry and never re-files under the wrong root.

suite_invariants: Every token is routed to exactly one expected_class — overlap (e.g. new_lemma AND new_root) is a differ bug.
No 'new_root' assertion ships radicals without QAC or human confirmation (confirm_root must be set); weak/hamza roots are never read off the surface.

Proclitics (بِ/وَ/فَ/ٱل), enclitics (ـهُ/ـكَ/ـنا), case-marking (tanwīn, iḍāfa kasra), and the tāʾ marbūṭa-as-suffix illusion are stripped/handled before classification — they must never leak into a headword.
Particles and pronoun-support constructions get root:null and go to the particle spine — never a content root.
occurrence_augment and new_surface MUST set no_new_entry/no-create — duplicating an existing lemma is a regression.
